qualitative.prompt: Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms.
qualitative.generation:
Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two particles become linked and their properties are correlated, even when they are separated by vast distances. This means that if one particle is measured to have a certain property, the other particle will instantly have that property, regardless of the distance between them.

This concept was first proposed by Albert Einstein as a way to explain why we don't see individual particles moving through space. However, it has been confirmed experimentally and is now widely accepted as a fundamental aspect of quantum mechanics.

In simple terms, quantum entanglement is a special type of quantum phenomenon where two particles become linked and their properties are correlated, even when they are separated by vast distances. This means that if one particle is measured to have a certain property, the other particle will instantly have that property, regardless of the distance between them.
